text: Did researchers just fake their findings? HARKing stands for Hypothesizing After Results are Known. It’s a sneaky way some scientists may distort truth. Imagine running an experiment, seeing unexpected results, and then changing your hypothesis to match. Sounds fishy, right? Studies show HARKing leads to inflated results and misinformed conclusions. The integrity of science hangs in the balance. Understanding HARKing helps us question research better and promotes genuine scientific inquiry. Stay curious! Share this video and learn more about science’s hidden truths.
